skill.name: backend-testing
skill.description: Create backend unit, integration, API, authentication, and database tests using frameworks such as Jest, Pytest, Mocha, Supertest, and FastAPI TestClient.
skill.summary: Build backend test suites for APIs, services, database logic, and authentication flows.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and environment access patterns, but review shows they are markdown examples for backend tests rather than executable skill code. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious package behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Trigger Static Detection
low_findings.description: The external command findings are inside fenced markdown examples, such as a test dependency installation command and test runner examples. They are user-visible setup instructions for normal backend testing, not hidden executable code in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are present, but semantic context shows documentation and sample commands. I found no executable script file or automatic command runner in the scanned skill files.

low_findings.title: Network And Environment Examples Are Test Documentation
low_findings.description: The fetch, HTTP request, URL, and SENDGRID_API_KEY references appear in examples for mocking an email service and testing API routes. The skill also instructs users to mock external APIs and avoid real external calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network and environment access strings are visible, but they are part of explanatory sample code. The surrounding instructions emphasize mocked dependencies and separate test environments.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Patterns Are Relative Imports And Test Environment Notes
low_findings.description: The path traversal and hidden file detections are caused by relative imports, related skill links, and .env.test guidance. These are common documentation patterns for test setup and do not demonstrate unauthorized file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path patterns are present in markdown examples and links. I found no code that reads arbitrary paths or accesses sensitive files at runtime.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis detected shell-like command text, including npm install and test scripts. Review found this pattern limited to markdown guidance for configuring test tools.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are fenced examples and are not invoked by packaged code. The residual risk is normal user-approved project setup activity.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Pattern In Mocking Example
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis detected fetch and HTTP request examples. Review found these examples demonstrate how to mock an email API and backend routes during tests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network strings are visible in sample application and test code only. The skill text explicitly recommends mocking external APIs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable Pattern In Example Code
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis detected an environment variable reference for SENDGRID_API_KEY. Review found it is used as a placeholder in a normal service example, not read by the skill itself.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The variable appears in documentation for user application code. No evidence found that the skill accesses or transmits marketplace user environment variables.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Pattern In Imports And Links
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis detected ../ path sequences in sample imports and related skill links. These are conventional relative references in markdown examples.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The locations are examples and documentation links. No file read, write, or traversal logic exists in the scanned skill files.

content.value_statement: Backend changes need tests that cover APIs, business logic, data access, and authentication paths. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create practical test plans, fixtures, mocks, and coverage checks.
actual_capabilities: Plans backend test coverage for APIs, services, database operations, and authentication flows.
Generates Jest, Pytest, Mocha, Supertest, and FastAPI TestClient test structures.
Recommends test database isolation, setup files, fixtures, and teardown patterns.
Shows how to mock external APIs, email services, file systems, and time-based behavior.
Defines coverage scripts, CI test commands, and failure troubleshooting guidance.
Provides examples for authorization, duplicate data, validation, and async failure cases.
limitations: It does not run tests unless the user asks the agent to execute commands.
It cannot guarantee compatibility with every project-specific framework configuration.
Example credentials and emails must be replaced with safe test-only values.
It focuses on backend testing and does not cover frontend component testing in depth.

use_cases.title: Add API Regression Tests
use_cases.description: Create success, validation, duplicate data, and authentication tests for new or changed REST endpoints.

use_cases.title: Prepare CI Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Define test scripts, coverage thresholds, and isolated test database setup for automated pipelines.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Bug Fixes
use_cases.description: Turn a reported backend defect into focused regression tests with fixtures and clear assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Unit Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for business logic coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this backend-testing skill to write unit tests for this service function. Include edge cases, clear test names, and arrange-act-assert structure.

prompt_templates.title: Test REST Endpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this backend-testing skill to create integration tests for these REST endpoints. Cover success, validation errors, missing data, and unauthorized requests.

prompt_templates.title: Design Isolated Database Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for data layer reliability
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this backend-testing skill to design tests for database operations. Include fixtures, transaction or migration cleanup, and assertions on stored records.

prompt_templates.title: Build A CI-Ready Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for full backend coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this backend-testing skill to produce a backend test strategy for this project. Include unit, integration, auth, mock, coverage, and CI execution guidance.

output_examples.input: Test an Express authentication API with register, login, and current-user endpoints.
output: A Jest and Supertest test suite outline for register and login flows.
Cases for duplicate email, weak password, missing fields, invalid password, and missing token.
Setup guidance for a separate test database and cleanup between tests.

output_examples.input: Add FastAPI tests for a user API that uses a database dependency.
output: A Pytest fixture plan using a test client and isolated database session.
Test cases for creation, duplicate records, login, and protected routes.
Notes for clearing dependency overrides and database state after each test.

output_examples.input: Prepare backend tests for CI with a coverage target.
output: Recommended test commands for normal, watch, coverage, and CI execution.
Coverage threshold guidance for statements, branches, functions, and lines.
Troubleshooting checks for open connections, shared state, and unhandled promises.

best_practices: Use a separate or in-memory test database and reset state before each test.
Mock external APIs, email services, file operations, and timers for deterministic tests.
Write clear test names that describe behavior, expected results, and failure conditions.
anti_patterns: Do not run tests against a production database or shared production services.
Do not depend on test execution order or state left by earlier tests.
Do not use real external network calls when a mock or stub can verify behavior.

faq.question: Which backend frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: It gives patterns for Express, FastAPI, Django, Spring Boot, and similar backend frameworks.

faq.question: Can it help with both unit and integration tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers isolated unit tests, API integration tests, database tests, and authentication flows.

faq.question: Does it require a specific test runner?
faq.answer: No. It includes guidance for Jest, Pytest, Mocha, JUnit-style tools, Supertest, and FastAPI TestClient.

faq.question: Will it call external APIs during tests?
faq.answer: The skill recommends mocking external APIs and avoiding real network calls during automated tests.

faq.question: Can it configure coverage checks?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides coverage threshold guidance and example commands for local and CI test runs.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security-sensitive authentication tests?
faq.answer: Yes, when used with test-only credentials, isolated data, and mocked external services.
